---
title: "Mary Emily Hullah"
---

## Mary Emily Hullah

**Dates:** (b. 1847/8)

### Description

novelist,  contributor to <i>MP</i> (1891)

### Biography

She was daughter of the musicologist John Pyke Hullah (1812-1884), a friend of Thomas Helmore and Derwent Coleridge;  in the 1881 census she was in London with her brother Francis, clerk to Lord Chief Justice Coleridge, and working as a governess; in the 1891 census she was staying with George Richmond the painter and described herself as an authoress.  She probably also contributed to the <i>Barnacle</i>.

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
